codeReview.summary: The fix adds real HTTP-boundary read checks for current timer and history list surfaces, but it does not fully cover the feature contract because the required owner-still-can-read assertion for history/entry-by-id in the same seeded scenario is incomplete.

issues.description: The feature contract requires owner-still-can-read assertions for the owner's own timer/history facts in the same seeded scenario. `TestSameWorkspaceOwnerCanStillReadOwnTimerViaHTTP` proves the attacker's own current timer and direct entry-by-id readback, but it never proves the victim/owner can still read their own history or entry-by-id facts through the HTTP boundary after the attacker-facing isolation checks. The feature's expectedBehavior explicitly includes owner can still read the owner's own timer/history facts, so this leaves the history half under-proven.

summary: Fail: the HTTP read-surface fix improves boundary coverage for current timer and history exclusion, but it still misses complete owner-still-can-read proof for history facts in the same scenario, so the feature contract is not fully satisfied.
